e2e_diagnostic.note: Pre-existing-suite ground-truth check performed before writing this story's new spec, to determine whether the returning-user identity signal (isFreshIdentity) needed special seeding. Ran the sibling prior-epic spec groups-invite-link.spec.ts (unmodified, out of this story's scope) against the current working tree via `E2E_GROUPS=1 node scripts/run-e2e.mjs tests/e2e/groups-invite-link.spec.ts`.
e2e_diagnostic.status: 2 of 11 tests FAILED (pre-existing, not introduced by this story)
e2e_diagnostic.detail: Both failures are `expect(getByTestId('join-request-card')).toBeVisible()` timeouts for a NAMED, boot-identity user opening a raw invite link. Root cause: S3's uncommitted groups.tsx:950 change narrows the full-screen JoinRequestCard branch to `isFreshIdentity === true`; every e2e-booted identity (via the addInitScript pattern all groups-*.spec.ts files share) already reads as non-fresh at mount, so these pre-existing specs — written before this epic's returning-user redesign — now hit the NEW list+banner landing instead of the full-screen card they assert. This is the epic's INTENDED behavior change surfacing as staleness in a prior epic's e2e spec, not a bug introduced by this story. Reported to the lead/architect as a cross-story finding (see result.json retrospective) rather than fixed here — out of this story's scope (production code and other spec files are both off-limits).

lint_detail: No .eslintrc*/eslint.config* present under app/ (next lint prompts for first-time interactive setup) — matches S1-S4's recorded NO_LINT baseline.
typecheck_detail: npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (app/) — zero errors at baseline (post S1-S4, pre-S5).
